title: Let's Measure Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The number 10, which is one whole group of ten ones.
rounds.explanation: ten: The number 10, which is one whole group of ten ones.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A shape with three straight sides and three pointed corners.
rounds.explanation: triangle: A shape with three straight sides and three pointed corners.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Large in size; it takes up a lot of space.
rounds.explanation: big: Large in size; it takes up a lot of space.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The number 20, which is two groups of ten ones.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A shape with four sides, where the two sides opposite each other match.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Little in size; it does not take up much space.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: There are ten fingers on my two hands.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My grandfather is seventy years old.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The roof has a triangle shape.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence compares the two animals correctly?
choices: A snake is longer than a worm.
A snake is more long than a worm.
A snake is long than a worm.
A snake is longest than a worm.
rounds.answer: A snake is longer than a worm.
rounds.explanation: Short describing words take -er rather than more, and a comparison of two things always needs than after the -er word.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses is or are correctly?
choices: There is ten circles on the chart.
There are one clock on the wall.
There are ten circles on the chart.
There is many circles on the chart.
rounds.answer: There are ten circles on the chart.
rounds.explanation: Ten is more than one, so the sentence needs are. There is only fits a single thing, such as one clock.

rounds.prompt: Choose the correct word: “I have ______ hands.”
rounds.explanation: Two is the number 2. Too means also, to is a joining word, and ate is the past form of eat.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What shape is the sun in this text, and what shape is a book?
choices: The sun is a circle and a book is a rectangle.
A new reading corner, with a mat long enough for six friends to sit on.
rounds.answer: The sun is a circle and a book is a rectangle.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: The sun is a circle and a book is a rectangle.

rounds.prompt: How far did Leo jump?
choices: Leo's feet are big and Amal's feet are small, so the same floor took a different number of feet.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: He jumped 45 centimetres.

rounds.prompt: What did Teacher Yasmin say they should use instead, and how many centimetres are in one metre?
choices: Leo's feet are big and Amal's feet are small, so the same floor took a different number of feet.
A ruler and a metre stick, and there are one hundred centimetres in one metre.
There are one hundred fingers in the air.
rounds.answer: A ruler and a metre stick, and there are one hundred centimetres in one metre.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: A ruler and a metre stick, and there are one hundred centimetres in one metre.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Count in Tens
rounds.target: Ten, twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, sixty, seventy, eighty, ninety, one hundred!

rounds.prompt: Speaking Game — Bigger, Longer, Taller
rounds.target: This pencil is longer than that pencil.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 - Shape and Pattern Talk
rounds.target: It goes circle, square, circle, square.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The elephant at the zoo is very big.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: A big stone is heavy to lift.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'pattern' mean in this unit?
choices: A design that repeats the same thing again and again.
The number 10, which is one whole group of ten ones.
The number 40, which is four groups of ten ones.
rounds.answer: A design that repeats the same thing again and again.
rounds.explanation: pattern: A design that repeats the same thing again and again.

rounds.prompt: What does 'short' mean in this unit?
choices: The number 10, which is one whole group of ten ones.
The number 40, which is four groups of ten ones.
Not long; it measures only a little from one end to the other.
rounds.answer: Not long; it measures only a little from one end to the other.
rounds.explanation: short: Not long; it measures only a little from one end to the other.

rounds.prompt: What does 'forty' mean in this unit?
choices: The number 70, which is seven groups of ten ones.
The number 40, which is four groups of ten ones.
The number 10, which is one whole group of ten ones.
rounds.answer: The number 40, which is four groups of ten ones.
rounds.explanation: forty: The number 40, which is four groups of ten ones.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'height'?
rounds.explanation: height is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'narrow'?
rounds.explanation: narrow is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'one hundred'?
rounds.explanation: one hundred is used as a number in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: The number 30, which is three groups of ten ones.
The number 70, which is seven groups of ten ones.
A round shape with no corners and no straight sides.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: A shape with two round tops and one point at the bottom.
A small unit of length; one hundred of them make one metre.
How heavy or how light something is when you lift it.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: Measuring a lot from one end to the other end.
Hard to lift because it weighs a lot.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: How many sides does a triangle have?
rounds.explanation: A triangle has three straight sides and three corners. A square and a rectangle each have four sides instead.

rounds.prompt: Look at this pattern: circle, square, triangle, circle, square, triangle. Which shape comes next?
rounds.explanation: The three shapes repeat in the same order, so after triangle the pattern begins again with circle.

rounds.prompt: How many centimetres are there in one metre?
rounds.explanation: Teacher Yasmin tells the class that one metre is always one hundred centimetres long.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: How many tens are there in one hundred?
rounds.explanation: One hundred is made of ten groups of ten, so the answer is ten. Five, twenty and one hundred are all real numbers, but none of them counts the tens.

rounds.prompt: Which number word comes straight after seventy when you count in tens?
rounds.explanation: Counting in tens runs seventy, eighty, ninety. Sixty comes earlier, seventeen is not a ten, and ninety comes one step later.

rounds.prompt: Which shape has no corners and no straight sides?
rounds.explanation: A circle is completely round. The other three shapes are all built from straight sides that meet at corners.
